\# Copilot Instructions for VisuaLume Project



\## Behavior Rules

\- Always read and understand existing files before writing any new code

\- Do NOT copy-paste entire files. Reimplement logic, improve structure, fix bugs along the way

\- Work on ONE task at a time. Never proceed to the next task until explicitly told to

\- After completing each task, summarize what you did and what the next task will be, then STOP and wait for user confirmation

\- Each task must end with a git commit using a clear, descriptive commit message



\## Code Standards

\- Use clean, readable JavaScript (ES6+)

\- Add comments to complex logic

\- Prefer CSS custom properties (variables) over hardcoded values

\- All interactive elements must be keyboard accessible

\- Validate and handle edge cases — do not assume clean input data



\## Project Context

\- This is a data visualization project built with vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript

\- Source of truth is C:\\Projects\\DV Project\\ (read this to understand what exists)

\- Target implementation goes into C:\\Projects\\DV Project\\project\\

\- The GitHub remote is https://github.com/Mojtaba-Alehosseini/VisuaLume



\## What to Check Per Task

1\. Does this code already exist in the source? If yes, understand it first

2\. Are there bugs or improvements to make? Fix them, don't blindly copy

3\. Does it conflict with other files? Resolve conflicts

4\. Is the CSS/JS correctly linked in index.html?



\## Git Rules

\- Use `git add` only for files changed in this task

\- Commit message format: `feat: \[short description]` or `fix: \[short description]`

\- Do NOT push unless the user says "push this"

\- Never create pull requests
